intro: Lasiorrhiza concinna, also known as the 'Golden Flower', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia. Traditionally, Lasiorrhiza concinna has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lasiorrhiza concinna include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lasiorrhiza concinna is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or extract, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to support skin and wound healing.
